beats.text: You learned to choose scales. You checked if the biggest number fits. You checked the last digits. Let's see what you know.

params.description: Vertical data table appears: Toys Collected. Amy: 45, Ben: 70, Cora: 15, Dan: 30.
Four scale options displayed below the data table. Scale of 1, 2, and 5 marked with ❌ and 'doesn't fit'. Scale of 10 marked with ✓.

beats.text: Four friends collected toys for a donation drive. Ben collected the most with 70 toys. Which scale works best for this data?
Which scale works best for this data?
Scale of 10. For data up to 70, Scale of 2 would need too many lines and Scale of 5 would need fewer, but still many lines. Scale of 10 only needs 8, much easier to read.

elements.description: Vertical data table showing Toys Collected values: Amy: 45, Ben: 70, Cora: 15, Dan: 30.
Bar graph with scale options displayed. Scale of 10 selected and confirmed.

params.description: Horizontal data table appears. Minutes Reading data. Gus: 23, Hal: 31, Eli: 9, Fay: 16.

beats.text: These students tracked how many minutes they read this week. Check the numbers: 23, 31, 9, 16. Which scale will show ALL these values exactly?
Which scale will show ALL these values exactly?

Scale of 2. The biggest value of 31 could work with Scale of 2 or Scale of 5. Then check those digits in the ones place: 3, 1, 9, 6. None are 0 or 5 so they aren't multiples of 5. That's your Scale of 2 signal to show the exact values on the graph.

elements.description: Horizontal data table. Minutes Reading data. Gus: 23, Hal: 31, Eli: 9, Fay: 16.

params.description: Vertical data table appears. Steps Walked data. Ana: 30, Ben: 70, Cara: 90, Dan: 50.

beats.text: All these numbers end in 0. Think about the biggest number.
Which scale is the best choice for this data?
options: Scale of 10 — it's easiest to read.
Scale of 5 — it shows more detail.
Scale of 2 — it works for any numbers.

condition.selected: Scale of 10 — it's easiest to read.

beats.text: Scale of 10. For data up to 90, Scale of 10 gives you a clean graph with only 10 lines on the axis.

condition.selected: Scale of 5 — it shows more detail.

beats.text: Scale of 5 could work, but the biggest number is 90. You'd need a lot of lines to reach 90. Scale of 10 only needs 10 — much cleaner for numbers this big.

condition.selected: Scale of 2 — it works for any numbers.

beats.text: Scale of 2 would need too many lines to reach 90! For big data like this, use a bigger scale.

elements.description: Vertical data table showing Steps Walked data. Ana: 30, Ben: 70, Cara: 90, Dan: 50.
